news.title: 2025 Taipei Medical Technology Exhibition | Successfully concluded, thank you to all the guests and partners who came
news.summary: The four-day exhibition ended successfully with a lot of communication, sharing and professional interaction. This year, we welcomed guests from many Southeast Asian countries to our booth, showing high interest in circulating tumor cells (CTCs) technology, making international precision medicine cooperation more in-depth and forward-looking. Good Future The special sharing by a strong team of doctoral students attracted many professionals...
body: The four-day exhibition ended successfully with a lot of communication, sharing and professional interaction.

This year, we welcomed guests from many Southeast Asian countries to our booth, showing high interest in circulating tumor cells (CTCs) technology, making international precision medicine cooperation more in-depth and forward-looking.
Good Future The topic sharing by a strong team of doctors attracted many professionals and representatives from medical institutions to listen.

The seats were full many times, and many doctors, technicians and researchers from the clinical side took the initiative to stay and communicate, asking in-depth about the clinical interpretation, technical details and application scenarios of CTC.
At the same time, it has also been highly recognized by many domestic medical teams, showing that CTC technology has obvious demand and development space on the clinical side.
International exchange is a highlight of this exhibition.

Thai YouTubers and Thai distribution partners also took the initiative to introduce the applications and advantages of CTC to Thai guests at the exhibition. At the same time, Dr. Lin also shared the value and technical features of CTC in cancer monitoring with Thai doctors, allowing more Southeast Asian medical units to see the depth and quality of Taiwan's technology.

Malaysian distributor WellSpring Solutions also communicated with visitors from Malaysia during the exhibition, sharing the clinical application results and cross-border introduction experience of CTC, allowing more international medical units to understand Taiwan's strength in the field of precision medicine.
Thank you very much to all the friends who came to the booth, communicated with us, asked questions and negotiated cooperation in the past four days.
We will continue to promote CTC and precision medicine-related testing services, and look forward to working with more clinical and international partners to promote medical innovation.
We look forward to seeing you all next time and working together for the advancement of medical technology!

news.title: [Media Reports] Exclusive/Inventec Smart Medical News, Executives Revealed Supply Chains in "These 2 Countries"
news.summary: Inventec (2356)'s Good Future, in response to the increasing demand for circulating tumor cells testing, has joined hands with its group partner Inventec Appliances to create the circulating tumor cells automatic testing system "Chiline CATCH"...
body: Good Future, a subsidiary of Inventec (2356), in order to respond to the increasing demand for circulating tumor cells testing, it has joined hands with its group partner Inventec Appliances to create a circulating tumor cells automatic testing system. Chiline CATCH", and Good Future Dr. Lin recently revealed that this system has been deployed in overseas markets and is gradually communicating with medical units in Thailand, Malaysia and other regions. It has a good chance of entering the medical supply chain of these two countries and comprehensively strengthening the group's medical layout in the Southeast Asian market.

Dr. Lin pointed out that in order to meet the huge domestic demand for tumor cell detection, the team is still racking its brains to continue to upgrade this automatic detection system. The latest CATCH HT and CATCH II models can detect up to 96 specimens per day and can simultaneously detect six biomarkers (biomakers). This greatly increases the detection energy and is expected to meet the market demand for such accurate and convenient rapid cancer detection.

Dr. Lin pointed out that the current detection range of this system includes the "ten major cancers". The most important thing is to test the blood of the subject. During the screening process, the red blood cells and white blood cells of the subject will be removed to avoid interfering with the test results. Through this system, it can be confirmed It can truly clarify the number of "tumor cells" contained in the test subject's blood, which is mainly divided into "healthy: 0-4 cells, medium risk: 5-29, high risk: more than 30 cells." Although the test cost is higher, the accuracy is better, so it has been cooperated by many institutions.

Dr. Lin said frankly that in addition to relying on the professional medical technology of the team, this system also requires the help of engineers from Inventec Appliances to jointly operate the system (CTC circulating tumor cells), so as to understand whether the tested people have tumors in their bodies. He said with a smile, "Ten years ago we said that computers would pick peanuts, but now computers can pick cells, and now they will help compile them into reports." Through three steps: early screening, target effectiveness evaluation, and postoperative follow-up, tumors and cancer cells can be effectively reduced, or the number of cells can be reduced.

Dr. Lin said that according to information released by the World Health Organization, only 5% of the world's truly healthy population is. In the face of the rapidly growing global demand for testing, Inventec Group has also entered the market through the circulating tumor cells (CTCs) automated testing equipment jointly developed by its two major subsidiaries. With the All-in-One design, it implements a fully automated testing mode of Sample-in and Data-out. It can obtain the CTC value in the blood and analyze the risk of cancer through blood testing, which has become a great pride of the biotechnology industry.

Dr. Lin revealed that many institutions, including Taipei General Hospital, have purchased GF_BRAND__'s system equipment, which can be used for people's specimens to be screened in the hospital, while other people and hospitals still have to send their specimens to Good Future's testing center for testing. However, as the number of cooperative testing units in the country continues to increase, it will be more convenient for people to use this system in the future. At present, this system is also being deployed in overseas markets. The recent targets are Thailand and Malaysia, two major markets. It is expected that there will be good news this year.

news.title: Inventec Appliances and Good Future "2023 Taiwan Medical Technology Exhibition" has achieved fruitful results
news.summary: The "2023 Taiwan Medical Technology Exhibition" was held grandly at the Nangang Exhibition Hall from November 30 to December 3. Inventec Appliances This year continues to focus on the three major medical health...
body: "2023 Taiwan Medical Technology Exhibition" was grandly held at the Nangang Exhibition Hall from November 30 to December 3. Inventec Appliances this year continues to focus on "Cloud Health Management-Family Pal Comprehensive Physiological Measurement System", "Smart Medical-TTSmart Infusion System" and "Precision Quasi-medicine - Chiline CATCHcirculating tumor cells automatic detection system" are three major medical and health products. With outstanding product and system maturity, they have won unanimous praise from domestic medical academics and industry professionals, as well as overseas customers from Poland, Thailand, United Arab Emirates, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, Vietnam and Indonesia.
The exhibition site and visitors in the exhibition hall

"Family Pal Comprehensive Physiological Measurement System" has been launched for nearly four years, and the product line has continued to expand. This year, the latest additions to the product series, "Body Composition Analyzer" and "Smart Belt" were exhibited. In addition to weight, the body composition analyzer can also measure the user's body fat percentage, BMI, body water, protein, bone mass, muscle mass, muscle rate, skeletal muscle mass, skeletal muscle rate, visceral fat, basal metabolism, body age and other data. It will be another tool for users to manage their health. The smart belt was jointly developed with the Taiwan Obesity Health Education Prevention and Control Association. It can detect the user's satiety and eating rate, which will bring diet (force-feeding) management to a new level of precision.

In addition to its domestic promotion, the "Family Pal All-round Physiological Measurement System" has also begun to move overseas. In addition to having obtained medical device certification in Thailand and Vietnam, the certification in Malaysia is also in full swing. We look forward to spreading it overseas in the near future to promote the excellent medical product system developed in Taiwan.

"TTSmart infusion system" is also a highlight of this exhibition. This type of smart infusion system will be an indispensable system for smart hospitals in the future. In the past, it was exclusively owned by large European and American manufacturers. Inventec Appliances is developed and produced by a local team in Taiwan "__TSMART __Infusion Pump" and "TTSmart Infusion Connection System Software" have broken the market monopoly. In addition to continuing to promote and use it with major hospitals in Taiwan, the "TTSmart Infusion System" has also received high praise from Thai customers at this exhibition, and is expected to start overseas expansion in the near future.

In addition to being used in medical institutions, the "TTSmart infusion system" is also suitable for Hospital at Home (HaH) situations. Infusions performed at home can be connected to medical institutions through the cloud, so that remote professional medical personnel can keep track of the home infusion status at any time. In addition to reducing the burden on medical personnel in medical institutions, it can also implement the goal of Hospital at Home (HaH).
Inventec Chairman Ye Guoyi (second from right) and the supervisor of the Health Strategy Council listened to the introduction of TTSmart system

"Chiline CATCHcirculating tumor cells automatic detection system" is also another highlight of this exhibition. In response to the increasing domestic circulating tumor cells testing demand, Inventec Appliances and its subsidiary Good Future exhibited the latest CATCH HT and CATCH Model II, each device can detect up to 96 specimens per day and can perform six biomarkers at the same time. This greatly increases the detection power and will meet the market demand for such accurate and convenient rapid cancer detection.
Good Future Dr. Lin (second from left) introduced "Chiline CATCH" to overseas customers present

This year Inventec Appliances, in addition to the above three major themes, also invites partners to simultaneously exhibit various related products and services. In terms of wound care, Inventec Group AI Center and Taipei Veterans General Hospital jointly developed the "Inventec" smart wound software that has recently obtained Level 2 medical material certification and Taiqiao Medical's "Pharmaceutical Electrospinning", which can be matched with each other as a complete solution for wound care.
Taiqiao medical staff (first from left) demonstrated "medical spinning" in wound care to the public

In addition, Xinsheng's remote health management services and Bio-Bio's "intestinal microbacteria testing services" and "specialized probiotics" have received enthusiastic responses from the public with the most complete product lines. Inventec Appliances relies on decades of experience in the research and development and manufacturing of information communication products, and is committed to the development and application of innovative medical products and services to promote human health and well-being.

news.title: [Media Report] Inventec Appliances has successfully laid out AIoT. Smart health housekeeper, infusion system, and CTC detection are eye-catching
news.summary: Inventec Appliances is a subsidiary founded by Inventec Group in 2000. Based on existing core technologies such as platform integration and cloud applications, it provides customers with new product development, design and manufacturing services; as the information and communications (ICT) industry enters the global trend of medical care, Inventec Appliances is also not absent...
body: Inventec Appliances is a subsidiary founded by Inventec Group in 2000. Based on existing core technologies such as platform integration and cloud applications, it provides customers with new product development, design and manufacturing services; in capital As the communications (ICT) industry enters the global trend of medical care, Inventec Appliances is also not absent. It is committed to developing medical-grade equipment products and connecting them with the medical Internet of Things (AIoT), hoping to improve medical efficiency and lead a healthy life. At this year's Taiwan International Medical Care Exhibition, Inventec Appliances also appeared with its own brand Chiline, and worked hand in hand with Xinsheng Health Management and Good Future to exhibit three main products and services: "Family Pal" smart health housekeeper, "TTSmart" infusion system, and "Chiline CATCH" circulating tumor cells (CTCs) automated testing services. On the first (8th) day of the event, it attracted the interest of many domestic and foreign exhibitors.
Comprehensive physiological measurement system to realize cloud health management

Zeng Qing'an, head of the medical business of Inventec GroupInventec Appliances, explained that "Family Pal" is a comprehensive physiological measurement system connected through the App. It integrates five physiological measurements of blood pressure, blood sugar, total cholesterol, uric acid and electrocardiogram to protect the health of your family in an all-round way. Currently, it includes: electrocardiograph, blood sugar/total cholesterol/uric acid monitoring system, blood oxygen/respiratory monitoring system, electronic sphygmomanometer, etc., all of which have obtained medical material licenses from the Food and Drug Administration of the Ministry of Health and Welfare. Other project products, such as continuous smart thermometers with Bluetooth/WiFi connection transmission functions, blood oxygen monitoring systems, etc., have also successively obtained certification and been launched on the market, expanding physiological monitoring to various fields. Zeng Qingan said that Inventec Appliances has successfully expanded the scope of Family Pal to the entire Taiwan through its extensive partner network. As for the App, Family Pal has more than 200,000 registered member accounts, and has cooperated with more than 20 hospitals, including National Taiwan University Hospital, Mackay Hospital, Linkou Chang Gung Memorial Hospital, Peking University Medical University, National Higher Education Medical University, Hualien Tzu Chi and other large hospitals, as well as more than 10 regional hospitals, and cooperates with more than 1,000 public (academic) associations, health centers, and clinics. In addition, Family Pal has also attracted five county and city governments to cooperate with it, including Miaoli County Social Affairs Department/Weigong Hospital, Tainan City Health Bureau, Yunlin County Health Bureau, Kinmen County Health Bureau, Taoyuan City Social Affairs Bureau; other types of partners include sunshine/long-term care centers, home care, insurance industry, academic research institutions, pharmacies, transportation industry, real estate industry, etc. In this year's exhibition, Inventec Appliances also focused on the urinary health problems of middle-aged and elderly men and exhibited the latest uroflowmeter. This product uses gravity sensing technology to measure the user's urine volume, flow rate, time, etc., and provides a reference for urologists' diagnosis. In early June this year, Inventec Appliances was also invited by the Peking University Affiliated Hospital's Rural Medical Group to travel to Shiding District, New Taipei City, to provide more complete detection of urinary problems for people in rural areas.
Taiwan's first smart infusion system to ensure patient safety

Zeng Qing'an introduced that the second key product on display this time is Taiwan's first "TTSmart infusion system", which includes an infusion pump, infusion connection system software, pump infusion set, etc., focusing on the high-risk infusion needs in the medical care field, and at the same time using a complete monitoring system to ensure the safety of patients' medical treatment. He said that the TTSmart infusion system can accurately control the infusion flow rate, with an error value of ±10% in the range of 0.1-0.9 ml/h; and an error value of ±5% in the range of 1-1000 ml/h. It can meet the infusion needs that require high precision, such as chemotherapy administration for cancer patients; drug administration records will also be automatically written into the hospital system, eliminating the need for manual transcription by medical staff. In addition, by connecting with the medical information system (HIS), this product can automatically compare doctor's order information, drug information, patient information, and pharmacopoeia database information to avoid human input errors and improve infusion safety. Information such as infusion status and alarms will also be automatically linked to the nursing station to facilitate real-time monitoring and emergency treatment by medical staff. Zeng Qing'an said that since the relevant information of the infusion process will be stored in real time, including the initial settings, who unlocked and modified it during the process, events (alarms) that have occurred, etc., will be completely recorded, so if a medical dispute occurs, it will be able to help trace the situation at that time.
CTC detection helps early detection of cancer, cross-industry cooperation jointly launches spillover insurance policies

In addition to medical care, Inventec Appliances cooperates with its subsidiary Good Future to step into precision medicine and launch the "Chiline CATCH" CTCcirculating tumor cells automatic detection service, which can be used in early cancer screening, treatment process tracking, tumor medication guidance, postoperative recurrence monitoring, etc. Zeng Qing'an said that this service only requires 2 mL of venous blood to detect more than 10 types of cancer, and because CTC can detect tumors six months earlier than traditional imaging, with a sensitivity of 90% and a specificity of 94%, it has an advantage in detection rate and can be used as an auxiliary tool for recurrence monitoring and treatment effect evaluation of cancer patients. In addition, Zeng Qing'an also emphasized that the negative screening and separation technology used by Chiline CATCH can more completely retain CTCs in blood for analysis than the positive screening and separation technology used by mainstream biotechnology manufacturers on the market. In addition, Chiline CATCH also has the advantages of no manual intervention in specimen pre-processing, fully automated processes, and the ability to connect to data systems and customized report formats according to hospital user needs, making it more convenient in practical operations. At present, Chiline CATCH's business model development, in addition to cooperation with medical institutions, can also be extended to high-end health examination services, and even cross-industry cooperation with insurance companies. For example, in January and March this year, Inventec Appliances successively announced the launch of spillover insurance policies with Global Life and Nanshan Life, hoping to allow policyholders to understand their own cancer risks in advance. Zeng Qing'an said that when Inventec Appliances was founded, it hoped to become the best partner in the research and development and manufacturing of smart handheld devices and cloud terminals through continuous innovation. After entering the medical care field, it also hopes to accelerate the layout of medical care products and go global through the sales base and cross-industry cooperation network established in Taiwan, coupled with the existing advantages of Inventec Group.

news.title: [Media Report] Inventec expands precision health territory and cooperates with Nanshan Life Insurance
news.summary: Good Future, a subsidiary of Inventec Group, has been committed to developing and promoting "circulating tumor cells test" (Circulation Tumor Cell test, hereinafter referred to as CTC test) since its establishment, and has actively expanded cross-industry cooperation. Recently, it has cooperated with Nanshan Life Insurance to promote spillover insurance policies, combining traditional oncology...
body: Good Future General Manager Zeng Qingan (fourth from left) and Nanshan Life Insurance Senior Vice President of Commodity Actuarial Chen Weixin (fourth from right).

Good Future, a subsidiary of Inventec (2356) Group, has been committed to developing and promoting "circulating tumor cells test" (Circulation Tumor Cell test, hereinafter referred to as CTC test) since its establishment, and has actively expanded cross-industry cooperation. Recently, it has cooperated with Nanshan Life Insurance’s health promotion spillover policy, combining traditional tumor biochemical indicators and advanced CTC detection services to help policyholders understand their own cancer risks in advance.

Cancer has always been the number one killer among the top ten causes of death, but there are no obvious symptoms in the early stage. Therefore, early detection and early treatment are particularly important. CTC detection is one of the powerful tools for early detection of cancer. circulating tumor cells (Circulation Tumor Cell, CTC) refers to cancer cells that enter the blood from the place where the tumor grows and circulate throughout the body with the blood. These cells circulate through the blood to other organs where they have the opportunity to grow into metastatic tumors.

In order to detect CTCs circulating in the blood, Good Future spent five years developing a fully automated circulating tumor cells detection system using exclusive negative screening technology. It can detect extremely rare CTCs in the blood and instantly reflect the risk of cancer. Therefore, CTC detection not only helps in the early detection and early treatment of cancer, but also can be used as an auxiliary detection during the treatment process and tracking. More importantly, only a small amount of blood is drawn during the detection process, so it does not have any side effects on the body.

Good Future General Manager Zeng Qingan said that since Good Future was recognized by the 17th National Innovation Award in 2020, it has not only successfully introduced CTC testing into major domestic medical centers and regional hospitals in the past few years, but has also actively expanded the cancer detection market at home and abroad. Good Future is cooperating with Nanshan Life Insurance’s health promotion spillover policy this time. The insured who has purchased this policy can enjoy free CTC testing services provided by Good Future to assess whether they have the risk of cancer, so as to achieve the purpose of personal health promotion and cancer prevention.

In addition, in order to improve the service experience of policyholders using CTC testing, Good Future has also cooperated with more than a hundred clinics and laboratories, so that policyholders across the province can complete CTC testing nearby.

Chen Weixin, senior vice president of commodity actuarial science at Nanshan Life, said that Nanshan Life's recently launched Nanshan Life Multi-Profit Rate Variable Whole Life Insurance (regular benefit type), in addition to providing life insurance protection, if the insured completes the CTC test within the time limit specified in the terms, he can enjoy the premium payable from the second policy year (excluding riders and additional terms) and endorsement terms) 2% discount to encourage the insured to use CTC detection technology and traditional health check-up tumor biochemical indicators as early as possible to have a more comprehensive understanding of whether they are at risk of cancer, thereby achieving the effect of early detection and treatment, and strengthening the awareness of self-care, which coincides with the health promotion concept that Nanshan Life is committed to promoting.

news.title: [Media reports] Inventec Group expands precision medicine service territory and joins hands with Global Life to launch health promotion spillover insurance policy
news.summary: Inventec Group Inventec Appliances's Good Future and Global Life announced a cross-industry cooperation to jointly launch spillover insurance policies that are in line with health promotion. Policyholders actively undergo cancer screening through Good Future's "circulating tumor cells test" (Circulating Tumor Cell test, hereinafter referred to as CTC test)...
body: Good Future General Manager Zeng Qingan (second from left) and Global Life Deputy General Manager Zheng Zhongan (second from right).

Inventec Group Inventec Appliances's Good Future and Global Life announced a cross-industry cooperation to jointly launch spillover insurance policies that are in line with health promotion. Policyholders can proactively undergo cancer screening through Good Future's "circulating tumor cells test" (Circulating Tumor Cell test, hereinafter referred to as CTC test), so that they can know as early as possible whether there is a risk of cancer, so as to achieve the purpose of early detection and early treatment, thereby achieving the benefit of promoting health.

According to the Ministry of Health and Welfare’s 110-year cause-of-death statistics in China, malignant tumors, commonly known as cancer, still rank first among the top ten causes of death in terms of mortality. Therefore, the National Health Department calls on the Chinese people: Cancer has no obvious symptoms in the early stages and is difficult to detect. Only early detection through cancer screening and early treatment can avoid greater subsequent medical expenses. With the rapid development of science and technology, there are more and more cancer screening tools and they are becoming easier. This time Good Future cooperates with Global Life Insurance. Through Good Future's exclusive negative screening technology, early screening of circulating tumor cells can be completed with a simple blood collection, which can monitor the risk of cancer and assist in early treatment and tracking to achieve the purpose of health promotion and cancer prevention.

Zeng Qing'an, general manager of Good Future and head of the electronic medical business of Inventec Appliances, said that when cancer cells enter the bloodstream from the growth site of primary tumors or metastatic tumors, they are called circulating tumor cells (CTCs). These CTCs will circulate throughout the body with the blood. At this time, if the immune cells in the body cannot recognize CTCs and destroy them, CTCs may take the opportunity to invade other organs, leading to the growth of metastatic tumors. It took Good Future five years to develop the fully automated circulating tumor cells detection system using exclusive negative screening technology. After receiving recognition from the 17th National Innovation Award in 2020, it has actively expanded the cancer detection market at home and abroad. This cooperation with Global Life to launch health promotion spillover policy products is a major milestone and affirmation for Good Future. It is hoped that CTC testing services will be more popular in the future and benefit more people.

Zheng Zhongan, senior deputy general manager of Global Life, said that through professional CTC testing, policyholders can not only enhance their awareness of self-care, but also learn whether they are at risk of cancer through the test results. For policyholders with test results showing a risk of cancer, early detection and early treatment can also be achieved. Therefore, in order to enhance policyholders’ awareness of self-care, Global Life provides all policies that meet the health promotion conditions designed by this policy. Because it helps reduce the risk of future policy claims, you can enjoy the spillover mechanism provided by this product, allowing policyholders to actively respond to the health promotion conditions designed by the product while planning for family protection, and achieve the purpose of personal health promotion and cancer prevention.

It only requires 2c.c. of blood to detect 21 common cancer types in China (such as lung cancer, colorectal cancer, breast cancer, etc.). The Pathology Laboratory Department of Taipei Veterans General Hospital purchased the IronMag®circulating tumor cells fully automatic testing system jointly developed by Inventec Appliances and Good Future in 2019. In the following year, an in-hospital self-funded project was launched, allowing cancer patients to use circulating tumor cells testing to track cancer and monitor treatment prognosis, making "Chiline CATCH" an industry-leading circulating tumor cells testing service.

Today (7/19) Good Future and Min'an Traditional Chinese Medicine signed the "Chiline CATCH" circulating tumor cells testing service cooperation agreement. From now on, people at Min'an Traditional Chinese Medicine can also have circulating tumor cells testing services that are equivalent to those of Taipei Wing General Medical Center.

Min'an Traditional Chinese Medicine's special services include traditional Chinese medicine cancer treatment, traditional Chinese medicine nephrology care, autonomic nervous system disorders, internal medicine (such as chronic diseases such as hyperglycemia, hyperlipidemia, gout and other chronic diseases) and acupuncture. The director, Dr. Li Ailing, has been working on integrated traditional Chinese and Western medicine for many years. For patients, the combination of traditional Chinese and Western medicine can multiply the therapeutic effects. In the field of cancer treatment, Dean Li also has rich clinical experience in treating cancer patients with different types of cancer (such as lung cancer, colorectal cancer, oral cancer, etc.) using the combined treatment of traditional Chinese and Western medicine. Dean Li proactively introduced Good Future "Chiline CATCH" as the cancer screening tool of Min'an Traditional Chinese Medicine. In addition to allowing the public to enjoy high-standard cancer detection services, Min'an Traditional Chinese Medicine also allows Min'an Traditional Chinese Medicine to strictly control the health of the people.

Good Future General Manager Zeng Qing'an said that Min'an Traditional Chinese Medicine has introduced "Chiline CATCH" circulating tumor cells testing service, which also shows that "Chiline CATCH" testing has a wide range of applications. It is not only highly accepted by Western medicine, but also favored by traditional Chinese medicine practitioners. In the foreseeable future, precision medicine combining Chinese and Western medicine will make cancer prevention and anti-cancer more comprehensive, and Good Future "Chiline CATCH" can also work with traditional Chinese medicine to protect people's health.

[Note] Liquid section: Using human body fluids (such as blood, urine, spinal fluid, etc.) as detection targets, compared with traditional tissue sections, it has the advantages of non-invasive, representative and instant detection, making it the best tool for auxiliary diagnosis.

news.title: New trends in cancer precision medicine: circulating tumor cells testing
news.summary: In the past, cancer diagnosis relied primarily on biopsy techniques. A single sampled tissue section cannot reflect the characteristics of the tumor in real time. Therefore, we are in great need of a safer detection method to obtain more comprehensive and dynamic information in the body to instantly reflect the development and treatment response of cancer. In recent years, scientists have gradually developed "liquid slices...
body: A new option for cancer precision medicine - liquid slice

In the past, cancer diagnosis relied primarily on biopsy techniques. A single sampled tissue section cannot reflect the characteristics of the tumor in real time. Therefore, we are in great need of a safer detection method to obtain more comprehensive and dynamic information in the body to instantly reflect the development and treatment response of cancer.

In recent years, scientists have gradually developed the new technology of "liquid biopsy". Liquid section refers to the analysis of biomarkers such as CTC_FULL__ (Circulating tumor cells, CTCs), circulating tumor DNA (Circulating tumor DNA, ctDNA), microRNA (miRNA), and extracellular vesicles (EVs) in peripheral blood or other body fluids. This method can avoid solid tumor puncture and can instantly reflect the heterogenous information of the tumor.
Figure 1. Introduction of liquid biopsy and application of circulating tumor cells

a. Liquid sections include: peripheral blood, hydropericardium, feces, urine, ascites, pleural hydrops, saliva and cerebrospinal fluid. Peripheral blood can be used to isolate circulating tumor cells, circulating tumor DNA, circulating tumor RNA and exosomes.

b. circulating tumor cells can be used in early diagnosis, prognosis prediction, disease tracking, molecular diagnosis and treatment response assessment. Image source: Circulating tumor cells in precision oncology: clinical applications in liquid biopsy and 3D organoid model. Cancer Cell International volume 19, Article number: 341 (2019)

The term "circulating tumor cells" was coined by Thomas Ashworth 150 years ago The doctor discovered that CTC refers to cancer cells that break away from the primary tumor and appear in the circulation system as single cells or aggregated clumps. Since then, medical reports have successively confirmed that as long as there are tumors in the body, there is a chance of metastasis. Each gram of cancer tissue releases 1 million fresh cancer cells into nearby blood vessels every day. As blood circulates throughout the body. These cancer cells that enter the blood circulation are CTCs (Figure 2).
Figure 2. The process of circulating tumor cells entering the blood circulation system
circulating tumor cells new detection technology

Since the number of CTCs in blood circulation is extremely small (only a few CTCs among billions of red and white blood cells), being able to effectively isolate CTCs is a huge technical challenge, so thorough and accurate detection methods are particularly important for further clinical applications.

CTC detection and enrichment technologies can be roughly divided into two categories. The first is the "forward screening" detection method, which mainly relies on antibodies to capture surface tumor antigens expressed on CTCs (meaning to select the fish species to be caught and the corresponding bait). Although antigen-antibody based CTC detection methods are relatively sensitive, they may miss a large group of CTCs with different characteristics due to the diversity of tumors. On the contrary, the detection method of "negative screening" is a size-based filtration method or immunomagnetic removal using antibodies calibrated to white blood cell markers to achieve the removal of main blood components such as red blood cells and white blood cells. The "negative screening" detection method that does not pre-select bait and fish species can retain a greater diversity of CTCs.
circulating tumor cells Scope of application of the test

In recent years, the clinical value of circulating tumor cells testing has gradually received attention, and many clinical studies have been carried out. These research results show that circulating tumor cells testing is an important diagnostic tool in cancer screening, disease tracking, etc. circulating tumor cells The applicable cancer types have almost covered the ten leading causes of death in China, including: rectal cancer, colon cancer, anal cancer, lung cancer (including bronchial and tracheal cancer), female breast cancer, liver cancer, intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma, gallbladder cancer, oral cancer, prostate cancer, thyroid cancer, skin cancer, stomach cancer, pancreatic cancer, esophageal cancer, kidney (including kidney cancer), bladder cancer, ovarian cancer, nasopharyngeal cancer, 21 types of cancer including cervical cancer, laryngeal cancer, and urinary tract cancer (including ureter and urethra).
Early screening application of circulating tumor cells detection

Several clinical studies have found that CTCs can be detected in early stages of tumor development. This approach has been demonstrated in a reported study on lung cancer, in which 168 patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease underwent annual helical computed tomography lung cancer surveillance, and all patients (five in total) with positive CTC counts developed lung cancer during their enrollment. In this study, CTCs were detected 1 to 4 years earlier than signs of malignancy detected by computed tomography. Therefore, CTC detection can realize the prospect of early cancer detection and early treatment, can also help distinguish malignant and benign lesions, and may become an effective biomarker to assist in the diagnosis of lung cancer.

Many basic health examination kits provide blood cancer screening. This type of tumor indicator is mainly used in clinical practice to evaluate the effect of cancer treatment and track recurrence. People will be very anxious once they find that the tumor index in the health examination is high. For example, CA-199 is a common tumor indicator for pancreatic cancer. According to large-scale studies, about 80% of patients with real pancreatic cancer have abnormal CA-199 index; however, among all groups with abnormal CA-199, the final chance of developing pancreatic cancer is less than one percent (0.5-0.9%)[2]. Different from general tumor biochemical indicators, CTCs are living tumor cells, which carry molecular and biological information of the entire tumor and can directly reflect changes in the tumor at various stages. The occurrence and progression of tumors can even be detected earlier before there are any signals on imaging. Therefore, for the above-mentioned people with abnormal CA-199 values, CTC testing can be used in the second line to decide whether to require more detailed related screening. People who are undergoing health checks with abnormal CA-199 and high CTC values ​​can choose painless gastroenteroscopy, ultrasound, MRI, etc. to further investigate whether there are abnormalities in the body; people who are undergoing health checks with abnormal CA-199 and CTC values ​​that are lower than normal can continue to track the changes in CA-199 and CTC values.
circulating tumor cells testing to evaluate treatment effectiveness

Clinical studies on breast cancer, lung cancer, colorectal cancer, and prostate cancer have shown that as an auxiliary diagnostic tool, CTC testing has good sensitivity (83-97%) and specificity (81-97%) [3-4]. Clinical studies on non-small cell lung cancer and metastatic cancer (breast cancer, prostate cancer, colorectal cancer) have shown that patients with lower numbers of CTCs in the blood will have better treatment effects. Even for patients with high CTC before treatment, if the CTC becomes lower during treatment, the disease development after treatment will be better in the future. Conversely, if the number of CTCs increases during treatment, future disease progression will be worse.
circulating tumor cells provides medication guidance

Since CTCs are cells that escape from tumor tissue into the blood, CTCs will carry the same genetic mutations as the original tumor tissue. Current clinical experimental results for lung cancer show that epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) gene mutations, which are common in lung cancer, can also be found in isolated CTCs, and this mutation is highly correlated with whether the use of targeted drugs is suitable. When resistance to targeted drug treatment occurs, scientists can also detect drug-resistant gene mutations from CTCs [5]. In the future, suitable target drugs can be found by analyzing the genetic mutations of CTCs, and the genetic mutations and quantitative changes of CTCs can be continuously tracked during the treatment process to understand the therapeutic effect of the target drugs in real time.
Which groups are suitable for circulating tumor cells testing?

For subjects, only a small amount of blood is drawn, and there is no need to fast beforehand. There is no need to worry about overexposure to radiation or allergies to developing agents that may be encountered in imaging examinations. CTC testing is a very convenient screening method. According to medical research, if the number of circulating tumor cells per milliliter of blood exceeds a certain range, it is highly suspected that a cancer is developing in the body. Cancer metastasis and treatment prognosis are also closely related to the number of CTCs. Therefore, cancer patients, those with a family history of cancer, those with high risk factors for cancer in their work or life routine (such as smoking, alcoholism, high stress, staying up late, or eating a greasy and low-vegetable diet for a long time, etc.), and those suspected of having cancer (such as recent weight loss, poor appetite, etc.) Poor health, or inexplicable pain that persists for a long time, etc.), and regular health check-ups. If the public can accept CTC testing, the effect of cancer screening and the quality of treatment and condition monitoring of cancer patients will be greatly improved, so that cancer will no longer be a terminal disease.
Vice President of Medical Care at Xiuchuan Hospital
Written by Dr. Lin Zhongwei, Vice President of Xiuchuan Cancer Center

[2] The clinical utility of serum CA 19-9 in the diagnosis, prognosis and management of pancreatic adenocarcinoma: An evidence based appraisal J Gastrointest Oncol. (2012) Jun; 3(2): 105–119.
[3] Novel Circulating Tumor Cell Assay for Detection of Colorectal Adenomas and Cancer. Clin Transl Gastroenterol. (2019) 10(10): e00088.
[4] Evaluation of the diagnostic value of circulating tumor cells with CytoSorter® CTC capture system in patients with breast cancer. Cancer Med. (2020) 9(5): 1638–1647.

news.title: Congratulations! Our company has been recognized by the "Digital Medical Industry Development and Cross-domain Promotion Plan" of the Industrial Bureau of the Ministry of Economic Affairs.
news.summary: The "circulating tumor cells automatic detection system for clinical evaluation of precision cancer diagnosis solutions" proposed by our company was recognized by the Industrial Bureau of the Ministry of Economic Affairs and received a subsidy from the "Digital Medical Industry Development and Cross-Domain Promotion Plan". The "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automatic detection system" jointly developed by our company and Inventec Group...
body: The "circulating tumor cells automatic detection system for clinical evaluation of precision cancer diagnosis solutions" project proposed by our company was recognized by the Industrial Bureau of the Ministry of Economic Affairs and received a subsidy from the "Digital Medical Industry Development and Cross-Domain Promotion Plan".

The "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automatic detection system" jointly developed by our company and Inventec Group is a fully automated circulating tumor cells detection system that can automatically complete procedures such as specimen processing, circulating tumor cells separation and staining, image analysis and report output. Testers only need to put blood samples, reagents and consumables into the device, and the system will automatically run and produce interpretation results.

The core technology of "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automatic detection system" is negative screening. Compared with the breadth of positive screening, negative screening can use specific biomarkers to capture various types of circulating tumor cells (Circulating Tumor Cell, Hereinafter referred to as CTC), it is supplemented by automated functions to achieve rapid detection, and can understand the dynamic changes in the course of cancer based on the increase or decrease in the number of CTCs.

The clinical trial of this project is to introduce the IronMag® automatic detection system into the auxiliary diagnostic evaluation of cancer and analyze the genetic variation of CTCs, in order to achieve the efficiency of rapid auxiliary diagnosis in the clinical diagnosis of different cancer types, and to assist doctors in choosing more accurate and effective treatment methods in medication guidelines.

Our company's general manager Zeng Qing'an said that through the clinical scientific verification of this project, we hope to increase the willingness to cooperate between medical institutions and biomedical related industries, and to enter the clinical testing and treatment market. In the future, an intelligent cancer precision detection platform can be developed. Through specific scientific detection data, treatment courses and medications can be customized to reduce ineffective treatments and achieve precision medical goals.

news.title: "Reprinted" The next step in the battle for technological supremacy. The new AI medical strategy of the seven electronic giants
news.summary: The reason why the smart medical industry attracts so much attention is that Taiwan's information communications and medical industries are both fields that are proud of the world. The "strong alliance" is naturally arousing expectations. Taiwan's electronics giants are competing to jump into the fray. After more than three years of exploration, they have each developed different strategies. Who is the current leader? ” In 2018, a new U.S....
body: The reason why the smart medical industry attracts so much attention is that Taiwan's information communications and medical industries are both fields that are proud of the world. The "strong alliance" is naturally arousing expectations. Taiwan's electronics giants are competing to jump into the fray. After more than three years of exploration, they have each developed different strategies. Who is the current leader?

In 2018, the ophthalmic AI software "IDx-DR" developed by the US startup IDx won the first AI medical software certificate from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and the world's first AI medical software certificate, marking the beginning of "certified" AI medical care. However, in 2000, Taiwan also issued the first license for ophthalmic smart medical materials, and the company that obtained this certificate was "Acer Smart Doctor", which is part of the Acer Group.
Acer has the most abundant medical evidence collection
ophthalmic AI software launched in Southeast Asia and received certification from three countries.

At present, Acer's ophthalmology AI software "VeriSee DR" and another telemedicine software have obtained two certifications from the Taiwan Food and Drug Administration (TFDA). Not only that, VeriSee DR has also obtained FDA certificates from Thailand, Indonesia, and the Philippines, making it the most prolific company in AI medical "certification" in Taiwan's electronics industry.

Why did Acer, which started out with desktop computers, laptops and other hardware, develop a software company with AI algorithms as its core competitiveness? This actually has a lot to do with Acer's "separation" 21 years ago.

In ○ 1 year, Acer started organizational restructuring and split the brand and OEM business. The former was operated by Acer, and the latter was undertaken by Wistron, which was established that year. At that time, although most of Acer's R&D personnel were assigned to Wistron, there were still more than 100 technical development talents who were sent to Acer's "Product Value Innovation Center" in Longtan, retaining the last trace of Acer's R&D incense.

This center, which was later moved to the Xizhi headquarters, and its “Forward-looking Technology Headquarters” responsible for software development, is the predecessor of Acer Intelligent Medicine. In 2016, artificial intelligence AlphaGo defeated the world chess champion, marking the arrival of the first year of AI. The team also plunged into AI and successively tried self-driving cars, robots, smart buildings, smart medical and other technologies. In 2018, the United States issued the first AI medical license, and they sensed the possibility of commercialization, and Acer Intelligent Medicine was established.

However, Acer Intelligent Medicine back then had actually been working with the National Taiwan University team for more than two years to develop a set of AI identification software focusing on retinopathy. Xu Yinxiong, general manager of the company, recalled that although both parties were dominant in technology and medical professions, it still took nearly four years to complete development and obtain certification when "no one had done it before."

The reason why it takes so long has something to do with both parties. On the hospital side, because National Taiwan University asked ophthalmologists to "support" this project outside of consultation time, Acer Intelligent Medical's engineers had to wait for the doctor's free time to "mark" the retinal image under the ophthalmoscope to determine whether it was a zero-level, first-level, second-level or fourth-level lesion before the data could be input into the AI ​​model. "So we often asked the doctors to spend more time to read the data!" said Xu Yinxiong.

As for Acer, what took the longest at that time was that they had to "self-study" a lot of knowledge about retinopathy. The reason for this is to prevent the interpretation of certain lesions from being a special case of "only one doctor thinks so", which ultimately leads to algorithm bias. Therefore, Acer's software engineers must have relevant medical foundations so that they can have the most immediate dialogue and reconfirmation with doctors when special conditions are discovered.

It took more than three years for this software to finally pass certification in 2000, but another challenge was about to begin. Because every time a hospital is developed, Acer Intelligent Medical's personnel have to "receive" problems from different units of the hospital. For example: doctors are concerned about "is the software accurate?", information staff are curious about "can the AI ​​software be integrated into our original system?", and procurement personnel ask Acer Intelligent Medical to propose different pricing methods for "buyout or lease?"

As of 2020, more than 50 medical institutions have purchased VeriSee DR from Acer Intelligent Medicine, 13 of which are medical centers. In 2021, the company's revenue reached nearly 10 million yuan, an increase of more than eight times compared with the previous year.

As its own business model of "development → evidence collection → promotion" becomes more and more proficient, Acer Intelligent Medicine will not only continue to develop independently in the future, but also plans to act as a "platform player" to commercialize the hospital's AI research results with commercial potential and promote them to the global market through the currently established hospital channels.
ASUS gave birth to another giant in the medical field
Collaborating with hospitals and Xinchuang to launch four major applications to enhance operations

Asus, which has been a "dead rival" with Acer in the laptop market for many years, has continued their past "giant lion strategy" in the motherboard market in the field of AI medical care - from front-end AI software development and medical speech recognition systems to middle-level AI development tools and computing power rental, all the way to the back-end, pursuing a medical information platform that can integrate data streams from different hospitals, like a lion opening its mouth, covering everything.

It is because of its ambition that Asus currently has five teams with more than 300 people working on the AI ​​medical field. Some of them even have team backgrounds that had nothing to do with medical care in the past. For example, the "Endoscopic Lesion Detection System" launched by ASUS at the end of 2011 and currently applying for Taiwan's TFDA certification was developed by a team from ASUS's graphics card department.

In addition to the above-mentioned front-end AI medical materials, Tai Zhiyun, which was established by ASUS in 2011 and is responsible for operating the state-owned supercomputer "Taishan No. 2", acts as an "ammunition depot" to provide medical AI startups with the rental of GPU (graphics processing unit) computing power. "Many new startups do not have so many resources to purchase very high-end computers and expand computing power. Tizhi ​​Cloud is their best support." said Wu Hanzhang, the company's trader and general manager of ASUS Health.

This business model is similar to the "AWS in the medical industry". In addition to the National Taiwan University Hospital, there are also several electronics companies that used to rank among the top five electronics companies with Asus, and even medical start-ups as far away as Singapore.

Currently, the largest of the five teams is the AI ​​Research and Development Center (AICS), which is in its fourth year of establishment and has more than a hundred people. The team is currently developing an ICD-10 (10th edition of the International Classification of Diseases) smart recommendation system that allows doctors to "speak" and the computer will automatically suggest the ICD-10 code to complete the health insurance payment declaration. So far, it has been adopted by 18 hospitals in Taiwan.

However, this recommendation system is still a "visible" AI application. Currently, the "Lumos Medical Big Data Platform" developed by ASUS AI Research Center seeks data exchange between different hospital systems, and can use AI to automatically read and manage medical records and reports, and provide real-time data on mutation trends, disease factors and other data.

This AI software, which focuses on back-end data integration and was developed in conjunction with the Hospital Affiliated to Sun Yat-Sen Medical University, is known within ASUS as the "information highway" in the medical field. Its purpose is to connect medical record data originally scattered in the "Health Information System (HIS)" of different hospitals to this unified platform for big data management and AI computing, solving the current problem of hospitals being unable to share data with each other due to different systems.
Wistron's four-way layout of preventive medicine
Promote a health care platform to use AI to help doctors and cure diseases
In terms of the fifth OEM, the one that is most similar to ASUS in its lion-like layout is undoubtedly the Wistron Group.

In 2016, at the request of Chairman Lin Xianming, all front-line managers of Wistron participated in the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) online course "The Business Strategy Implications of AI." The course focused the commercialization of AI in the four fields of natural language, image recognition, robotics, and big data analysis, which has affected Wistron's AI medical layout to date.

That year was also the year that Wistron established "Wistron Medical". The company's remote wound care system, medical AI voice platform, and ICD-10 intelligent recommendation currently used by Enzhu Public Hospital and Kaohsiung Veterans General Hospital correspond to the three fields of image recognition, natural language, and big data analysis.

In addition, Wistron Medical's "BestShape Chronic Kidney Care Solution" currently cooperating with National Taiwan University Smart Living Center is trying to expand its use from hospitals to dialysis clinics. It uses AI to connect kidney patient information from different institutions to carry out case management and low blood pressure warnings for terminal patients, similar to ASUS's "information superhighway" concept.

Not only that, Wistron has also developed a health management platform Health 365, which allows patients and their families to manage their own health. At present, this system has been introduced by Kaohsiung Municipal Xiaogang Hospital, Taipei Dialysis Clinic, and even companies and health examination centers use it for the health management of employees and members.
Quanta creates the largest medical cloud in Taiwan
creates the first auxiliary diagnostic system to identify people with potential depression

As for Compal, the layout that focuses most on AI medical care so far is Hongzhi Biomedical, which was established in 2019 and focuses on brainwave frequency analysis. Currently, the company's hardware "brain wave meter" has obtained certification from the US FDA and Taiwan's TFDA, and its AI software "brain wave stress index assessment system" will also submit applications to the Ministry of Health and Welfare for certification in the first half of 2020.

The core technology of this company, 90% owned by Compal, is "brain wave signal processing". It is developed by Liu Yihong, a professor at the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Taipei University of Technology who has been researching in this field for more than ten years. The value of this AI technology that integrates software and hardware is that in the past, when psychiatrists judged depression, they used their own clinical experience, supplemented by assessment scales similar to examination papers. In the future, with brainwave AI analysis, it will be able to provide doctors with analysis of depression based on physiological data.

The only difference from other industries is that there is no ready-made database for brainwave data. Therefore, in the data collection stage before training the AI ​​algorithm, Hongzhi Biomedicine, "We collect the data one by one through research projects with different units. Unlike X-ray and MRI (nuclear magnetic resonance), which have ready-made and huge databases, developers only need to mark them." The company's chief technology officer Weng Changxin revealed.

Therefore, Hongzhi Biomedicine spent a whole year, and it was not until January 2022 that the 500 pieces of data were completely collected. In the future, after obtaining certification, they will not only promote it to psychiatric departments in hospitals, but also include health examination centers, sleep centers, community clinics, etc., to help these institutions identify potential depression groups.
Inventec develops new anti-cancer tools
Liquid biopsy detection makes 80% of cancer cells invisible

As for Inventec, it regards AI medical software as a tool for value-added and differentiated hardware. For example, the circulating tumor cells detection machine jointly launched by the company and Good Future and calling for "liquid pathology slides" is equipped with Inventec Appliances, a subsidiary of Inventec, which can use AI to analyze cancer cell detection software in the blood circulation.

The system, which has been developed for six years and has an accuracy of more than 80%, has been introduced by the Pathological Examination Department of Taipei General Hospital. Each test costs about 15,000 yuan, which is at least half cheaper than traditional imported equipment.

Looking at the AI ​​medical business of Electronic Fifth Brother, most of them are still in the stage of not yet making a profit, but for the future, by first obtaining regulatory certification from various countries, then promoting it at the hospital, and finally expanding the customer scale, this will be the common path for these major manufacturers to "monetize" AI and then make profits.
Source----
"Today's Weekly" Magazine / Issue 1308 / Issued in January 2022

body: The Health Policy Association held a meeting of directors and supervisors on the 19th and invited Inventec Group Chairman Ye Guoyi, Fubon Financial Holdings Chairman Tsai Ming-hsing, and Taishin Financial Holdings Chairman Wu Dongliang to serve as consultants to the association. news.title: [Media Report] Good Future attacks preventive medicine and uses automated detection to detect cancer cells
news.summary: Inventec Appliances, a major electronics manufacturer with more than 26,000 employees around the world, chose to cooperate with a new start-up company Good Future in order to enter the biomedical field to jointly develop the most advanced cancer screening automation system in Taiwan. Good Future, this small company located in Guishan, Taoyuan, is ushering in a new era of cancer medical testing in Taiwan. (Extended reading: Honor…
body: Good Future Operations Director Hong Weishan (from left), Chang Gung Memorial University Professor Zeng Qingping from the Department of Medical Technology, and Inventec Appliances Medical Electronics Business Operations Manager Zeng Qingan. Reporter Xie Baihong/Photography

Inventec Appliances, a major electronics manufacturer with more than 26,000 employees around the world, chose to cooperate with a new start-up company Good Future in order to enter the biomedical field to jointly develop the most advanced cancer screening automation system in Taiwan. Good Future, this small company located in Guishan, Taoyuan, is ushering in a new era of cancer medical testing in Taiwan. (Extended reading: Good Future’s exclusive technology can screen 80% of tumors) On December 1 last year, Inventec Appliances and Good Future, after three years of cooperation, won the “17th National Innovation Award” in the Biopharmaceutical and Precision Medicine Category of the Enterprise Startup Group with an automated detection system named “IronMag®circulating tumor cells”.

Inventec Appliances is a subsidiary of Inventec Group (2356), the first brand of handheld electronic products in the Chinese world. Its "OKWAP" products have always been famous for their innovation and beauty in the market, covering various possible needs for network communication in the entire Greater China market. But the outside world is very curious, why did Inventec Appliances enter the field of cancer precision medical testing? What kind of company is Good Future that cooperates with Inventec Appliances?
Innovative approaches to make costs more affordable

Inventec Appliances Deputy General Manager Zeng Qing'an said that the group's development strategy in the biomedical field in recent years has focused on the three major fields of "smart medical care", "cloud health management" and "precision medicine". The first two products developed are the "Family Pal" physiological measurement system and the second "TTSmart" Zhihui infusion system. Both of them are developed independently. The third "precision medicine" part is through the cooperation with Good Future.

Zeng Qing'an said that "Family Pal" is a comprehensive physiological measurement system launched early last year; "TTSmart" smart infusion pump combines medical Internet technology with the purpose of assisting medical staff to complete infusion for patients more accurately, providing safety and simplicity.

As for the "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automatic detection system" cooperated by Inventec Appliances and Good Future, it is a set of automated equipment for cancer detection, a technology developed by the research team of Zeng Qingping, a professor at the Department of Medical Technology of Chang Gung Memorial University. The so-called "circulating tumor cells (circulating tumor cells, CTC)" means that cancer cells break off from the primary or early metastasis site and enter the blood vessels, and then further migrate to more parts of the body through blood circulation. A larger number of CTCs usually means the cancer is more severe.

Good Future Operations Director Hong Weishan said that among cancer types, 80 to 90% of cells are epithelial cell cancers. For example, lung cancer, breast cancer, and colorectal cancer are the most common epithelial cell cancers. Good Future is extracted from the blood of the human body to detect epithelial cell cancers circulating in the body.
Inventec Appliances cooperated with Good Future to develop "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automated detection system" and won the 17th National Innovation Award. Good Future／Provided
Target business opportunities and expand mainland territory

Inventec Appliances cooperates with Good Future to develop an automated testing system using CTC as the testing project. Zeng Qingping explained that currently, it often takes several days to test CTC in medical institutions or laboratories from the time the sample is obtained to the release of the test report. However, through Good Future's automated system, eight case tests can be completed in one day, and only 2cc of blood can detect 80% of cancer cells.

In 2017, Inventec Appliances, a subsidiary of Inventec Group, inadvertently learned about Zeng Qingping's technical principles in order to find suitable cancer detection technology. It can convert CTC detection from manual to fully automatic detection. Not only can the detection time be shortened, but the detection cost can also be significantly reduced, from 30,000 yuan per time to half.

However, many medical centers in China currently have CTC detection functions. The "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automatic detection system" jointly launched by Inventec Appliances and Good Future, compared with other detection systems, what are the functional differences? Hong Weishan explained that the CTC tests currently conducted by various medical centers adopt a "forward screening" mechanism, which is to find possible cancer cells in the blood. The accuracy is low and the possibility of false negatives (cancer is present but not found) is high. However, Inventec Appliances and Good Future have developed a "negative screening" mode detection system. Through the elimination method, the process of lysing red blood cells and removing white blood cells, and then identifying CTC functional markers, can more accurately calculate and capture cancer cells.

This CTC detection system has been approved by two large medical centers in the north and has been purchased by the medical center. Zeng Qing'an emphasized that since various medical institutions in mainland China are also very popular for cancer detection, this system will also begin to develop into the mainland market. It has obtained a mainland patent, and business opportunities to open up the international market for precision medicine are just around the corner.

news.title: [Media reports] Inventec Appliances and Good Future Upgraded cancer precision medicine technology won the National Innovation Award
news.summary: Inventec Appliances, a subsidiary of Inventec Group (2356), cooperated with Good Future to jointly develop the "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automated detection system" for liquid slice detection technology in the field of cancer precision medicine. On December 1, it won the 17th National Innovation Award in the Biopharmaceutical and Precision Medicine Category of the Enterprise Startup Group...
body: The "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automated detection system" jointly developed by Inventec Appliances under Inventec and Good Future won the 17th National Innovation Award. Photo / provided by Inventec Appliances

Inventec Appliances, a subsidiary of Inventec Group (2356), cooperated with Good Future to jointly develop the "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automated detection system" for liquid slice detection technology in the field of cancer precision medicine. On December 1, it won the Biopharmaceutical and Precision Medicine category of the Enterprise Startup Group of the 17th National Innovation Award.

Liquid sectioning is a new strategy for cancer precision medicine. Detecting the presence of circulating tumor cells in the blood can provide the most direct evidence of cancer detection, "seeing is believing". Clinical detection is very important in all stages of cancer.

It is a pity that the existing detection platforms on the market are cumbersome, time-consuming, lack fully automated detection and other factors, which makes it impossible to carry out circulating tumor cells detection in clinical practice. This prevents cancer patients from getting more complete information on cancer changes. Breaking through this bottleneck is a top priority.

Inventec Appliances and Good Future adopted a different strategy from existing products on the market and used the "negative screening" mode to design the "IronMag®circulating tumor cells fully automatic detection system" product. By matching a dedicated reagent and consumable set, the system integrates circulating tumor cells's separation, immunofluorescence staining, cell image acquisition, AI-assisted image interpretation, manual review, automatic report output and other processes, greatly improving the accuracy and quality of circulating tumor cells detection.

Good Future pointed out that the "IronMag®circulating tumor cells automated detection system" is based on the negative screening mode that has been verified by hundreds of clinical cases of cancer patients. With the core design concept of all-in-one, it implements the fully automated circulating tumor cells detection mode of sample-in and data-out.

In addition to solving the bottleneck of most existing circulating tumor cells detection being unable to replace manual operations with fully automated mode, this system can stably separate trace amounts of circulating tumor cells from whole blood and has high detection specificity and sensitivity. It will also provide a new detection platform for the cancer precision medicine industry and the liquid slice application market.

share.title: circulating tumor cells Detection: A new trend in precision cancer medicine
share.summary: Observe CTCs directly from liquid sections, cancer cell transfer, and into the blood to understand why circulating tumor cells testing is becoming an important tool for cancer risk assessment and tracking.
body: Most common cancers originate from epithelial cells. When tumors continue to grow, some cancer cells will break away from the original site and enter the blood circulation, forming circulating tumor cells (CTCs). These cells are important clues to cancer metastasis and recurrence. They are also the most direct evidence in liquid sections that can be observed at the cellular level.

In the past, clinical practice mostly relied on imaging examinations to understand tumor changes. However, imaging tools may still need to be supplemented with other more sensitive information for very early, small, or dynamically changing lesions. Liquid sectioning has therefore developed rapidly, and in different detection pathways such as circulating tumor cells, circulating tumor DNA and exosomes, the value of CTC lies in the ability to see the cells themselves, not just speculative molecular signals.

It is not easy to find small and highly heterogeneous amounts of circulating tumor cells in the blood. The actual detection process usually includes cell enrichment, immunofluorescence staining, image acquisition, and cell identification and counting, each of which affects subsequent interpretation. This is why Rongwen designed the test into a systematic process. The purpose is not to obtain a single number, but to retain cell images and marker evidence that can be interpreted later.

For subjects, the circulating tumor cells test also has the convenience of liquid slices. It can be done with a blood sample, without the need to fast beforehand or the risks of radiation exposure or contrast agents that are common with imaging tests. When these features are combined with readable cell images and report content, CTC testing is not just a single test, but an information tool that is more suitable for inclusion in risk assessment, treatment tracking and follow-up communication processes.

share.title: Exclusive negative filtering technology: retain more circulating tumor cells types
share.summary: From blood cell removal, white blood cell identification to PowerMag magnetic column processing, understand why Rongwen uses negative screening to reduce the loss caused by a single marker.
body: circulating tumor cells can be roughly divided into two paths that rely on physical properties and those that rely on biological properties. The former uses differences in cell size and chargeability for separation, while the latter identifies based on surface protein expression. The problem is that tumor cells themselves are highly heterogeneous, and if you only rely on a single condition for capture, there is often a risk of missing out.

The so-called forward screening is to first find cells that match specific tumor markers and capture them. This method is relatively straightforward when the marker expression is stable, but once the tumor cells undergo phenotypic changes during the metastasis process, or are not originally a population that expresses the marker highly, it may not be fully retained. The enrichment methods of

The core idea adopted by Rongwen is negative screening. The actual process will first use the difference in osmotic pressure to remove red blood cells, then use specific antibodies to identify white blood cells, and use the PowerMag magnetic column to remove interfering blood cells from the blood, retaining non-blood cells as much as possible. This kind of design does not first assume that tumor cells must grow in a certain way, but first eliminates unnecessary cells and then retains more targets that may be meaningful.

When immunofluorescence staining, automated microscopic photography, and cell image interpretation are subsequently added, the advantages of negative screening are truly amplified. It allows the back-end report to not only be determined by a single signal, but to retain more complete cell images, staining combinations and interpretation basis, reducing leakage and misjudgment caused by only looking at a single marker.

share.title: Both epithelial and mesenchymal types are important: CTC detection should not only look at a single signal
share.summary: Cancer cells will undergo phenotypic conversion during the metastasis process. If we only look at a single positive signal, we may ignore more risky cell populations.
body: circulating tumor cells does not have only one fixed appearance. Many cancers originally originate from epithelial cells, but in the process of breaking away from the primary focus, entering the blood circulation, and preparing for distant metastasis, the cells may gradually develop epithelial, mesenchymal, or even mixed phenotypes. This is also a phenomenon commonly referred to clinically as phenotypic switching or EMT.

Once a detection pipeline relies solely on a single forward marker to enrich cells, it is easy to focus on populations with high expression of specific surface proteins and ignore cells that are already in transition. For cancer monitoring, what really needs to be grasped is often not the most typical group of cells, but the changing groups that are undergoing metastasis and adaptation.

Rongwen integrated cell enrichment, immunofluorescence staining, and image interpretation into an integrated process to avoid leaving only a single signal in the result. When cell images, staining performance and counting results can be viewed together, the report is not just yes or no, but closer to what is actually happening in the sample, making it easier to further understand cell types and marker combinations.

For situations that require risk assessment, treatment tracking, or communication with the medical team, this method of incorporating different phenotypes and cell images is more in line with the information density that precision medicine really requires. Detection is not just about finding the cells that are easiest to find, but trying to preserve the cells that really deserve attention.

share.title: 2 mL blood test report within 5 business days: How does Rongwen’s testing process work?
share.summary: From 2 mL blood collection and pre-processing to complete cell imaging reports, understand how Rongwen strings together the process into readable test results.
body: For most subjects, the first difference in the testing experience is not the instrument, but the method of collection. The circulating tumor cells test can be completed with a small amount of blood sampling, without fasting in advance and without the burden of radiation or contrast agents that are common in imaging examinations. Rongwen currently uses 2 mL blood samples as the starting point for testing, making it easier to incorporate testing into daily health management, follow-up follow-up and treatment observation processes.

After the sample enters the laboratory, the result is not obtained directly, but pre-processing, cell enrichment, immunofluorescence staining, automatic microscopic photography and image interpretation are completed in sequence. If these steps are scattered with each other, it will be difficult to stably compare them; Rongwen strings them into a continuous process in order to reduce manual differences and keep the signals and images of the same sample in the same detection context.

Reports are usually delivered within 5 business days and provide not only numerical values ​​but also complete cell images, interpretation markers and basic instructions. This is where the circulating tumor cells test is really valuable: you don’t just see a few cells, but you can go back to the cell level to understand the results and know what happened in the sample.

For people who need to track over time or communicate with their medical team, a readable test report is far more important than a single number. When the report has consistency in images, labels and processes, subsequent discussions will be more based and the test results can be better understood in real clinical and health management scenarios.
